title: Smoky Creamy Tahini Stir-fry
description: A weeknight vegan-friendly Korean dish featuring smoky flavors and creamy tahini sauce, perfect for quick meal prep.

ingredients.name: 2 tablespoons tahini
ingredients.notes: A vegan-friendly alternative to soy sauce

ingredients.name: 1/4 cup water
ingredients.notes: Adjust as needed for desired consistency of sauce

ingredients.name: 1 bell pepper, diced (any color)
ingredients.notes: Add a pop of color and sweetness to the dish

instructions: Preheat your wok or large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add toasted sesame seeds and a pinch of salt to the hot pan. Toast until fragrant and lightly golden, about 1-2 minutes.
Melt 1 tablespoon of coconut oil in the same pan if using, then add minced garlic and smoked paprika. Sauté for another minute or two until fragrant.
Pour in tahini, water, lemon juice (if using), ginger, nutritional yeast (if using), and soy sauce (or tamari). Stir to combine and bring to a simmer.

Once the liquid is thickened into a smooth sauce, add diced bell pepper and sliced onion. Stir frequently until all vegetables are heated through and the sauce has slightly thickened, about 5-7 minutes.
Season with salt and pepper to taste before serving. Enjoy as a side dish or over rice for an authentic Korean flavor experience.
